_meta.tests_invariant: no X-rule firing is silently dropped; firings with affected_domain not in proposal set are recorded in synthesis_meta.notes as orphan_firing_<rule_id>
_meta.scenario_in_one_sentence: Stress is high today, firing X7, but no stress proposal was submitted — synthesis must not silently drop the firing

proposals.rationale: Readiness adequate, sleep low-debt, ACWR 0.90 — green for a hard session.
Fresh; weekly mileage in normal range; last hard session 2 days ago.

expected_x_rule_firings.note: stress.composite_stress_state=high — cap confidence on all recommendations to moderate

note_on_orphan: X7's affected_domain is 'stress' in this firing, but there is no stress proposal in the proposal set. The orphan case per the plan is 'a firing whose affected_domain is not present in the proposal set'. In X7's literal spec ('cap confidence on all recommendations'), all-domain caps could also apply to recovery+running without stress present — but the firing payload above claims affected_domain=stress, mirroring how the runtime would emit it. Either interpretation exercises the same invariant: the firing must not be silently dropped just because no matching proposal exists.
pass_criteria: Synthesis must surface the X7 firing somewhere in the output — either in synthesis_meta.notes (plan text) or by applying the cap to the present domains and logging the rule_id
If the skill cannot route the firing because 'stress' is not in the proposal set, it must record 'orphan_firing_X7' (or equivalent tag) in synthesis_meta.notes
Under no circumstance should the final plan be two un-capped high-confidence recommendations with no mention of X7
